title: Allocate memory buffer without zeroing — bump free pointer, skip MSTORE initialization
description: Solidity's new T[](n) zeroes every slot before returning. For temporary buffers where every slot will be written before read, zeroing is wasted work. Manual allocation just bumps the free pointer: buffer = mload(0x40); mstore(0x40, buffer + 32*(n+1)). Caller must write all slots.
pattern_before:
// Solidity zeros n*32 bytes
bytes32[] memory buf = new bytes32[](n);
when_to_apply: Temporary scratch buffers where all n slots are written before being read or returned
when_not_to_apply: Buffers returned externally, partially written buffers, any case where uninitialized values could be read
